spec_match.rationale: The demo card has no actual CSS styles implementing anchor positioning under @supports named-feature(), making the enhanced and fallback modes behave identically.

interactive.rationale: The page has hover states to show tooltips, but does not display any real behavior change under Chrome 150 since the anchor positioning CSS is completely missing.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb accurately describes a progressive enhancement layout using named-feature to opt into transforms-tracked anchor positioning.

edge_cases.rationale: The page fails to demonstrate the actual capability because it only includes the progressive enhancement CSS in a text display card, not in the active <style> block.

references.rationale: Includes a ChromeStatus reference link under the 'see also' section.

design_system.rationale: Uses the showcase design system, featuring two clean comparison columns with monospace code snippets.

openQuestions.title: Implement the progressive enhancement CSS in the active style block
openQuestions.detail: The page shows the CSS for @supports named-feature("anchor-position-follows-transforms") in the code comparison container, but does not actually declare it inside the document <style>. Add the real anchor-positioning rules within an @supports named-feature("anchor-position-follows-transforms") block so that browsers with the feature active actually render the tooltip anchored to the icon via anchor-positioning rather than using simple relative offsets.
